monsters.name: Goblin
monsters.tactics: Uses Nimble Escape to disengage or hide as a bonus action. Attacks from ambush, retreats when outnumbered. Fights in groups of 4-8.
specialAbilities: Nimble Escape: Disengage or Hide as bonus action

monsters.name: Skeleton
monsters.tactics: Mindless undead, follows last order. Ranged skeletons stay at distance; melee skeletons rush in. Vulnerable to bludgeoning.

monsters.name: Zombie
monsters.tactics: Shambling, relentless. Undead Fortitude keeps them standing. Slow but durable — they absorb hits that would kill living creatures.
specialAbilities: Undead Fortitude: On dropping to 0 HP, Con save DC 5 + damage taken to drop to 1 HP instead. Fails on radiant/critical

monsters.name: Orc
monsters.tactics: Aggressive — charges closest enemy using Aggressive trait to close distance. Prefers melee. Retreats only when leader is killed.
specialAbilities: Aggressive: Bonus action to move up to speed toward hostile creature

monsters.name: Bugbear
monsters.tactics: Ambush predator. Surprise attack deals extra 2d6 damage. Uses Stealth to set up first-strike advantage. Leads goblin ambushes.
specialAbilities: Surprise Attack: +2d6 damage on first hit against surprised creature
Brute: Melee weapons use d8 instead of d6 for damage

monsters.name: Owlbear
monsters.tactics: Territorial and aggressive. Multiattack with beak and claws. Charges nearest threat. Will fight to the death defending young.

monsters.name: Troll
monsters.tactics: Relentless brawler. Regenerates 10 HP per turn. Multiattack with claws and bite. Fools rush in without fire or acid — trolls regenerate from anything else.
monsters.tacticsKey: Fire or acid stops regeneration. Without these, trolls are nearly unkillable.
specialAbilities: Regeneration: Regains 10 HP at start of turn. Does not regenerate if takes fire/acid damage. Dies only if starts turn with 0 HP and can't regenerate.

monsters.name: Young Green Dragon
monsters.tactics: Intelligent, deceptive, and patient. Prefers to negotiate from advantage before attacking. Uses breath weapon on clustered groups. Retreats if outmatched to plan revenge.
specialAbilities: Amphibious: Can breathe air and water
Poison Breath: Recharge 5-6

monsters.name: Mind Flayer
monsters.tactics: Uses Mind Blast to stun groups, then extracts brains from stunned targets. Leads with psionics, uses tentacles on isolated targets. Commands thralls intelligently.

attacks.name: Tentacles
attacks.damage: 1d8 + 4 psychic (grapples on hit, target stunned if already grappled and hit again)

attacks.name: Extract Brain
attacks.damage: 10d10 psychic (target must be grappled, incapacitated, or stunned; kills on reducing to 0)

attacks.name: Mind Blast
attacks.damage: 4d8 psychic (stunned on fail, half on save)

specialAbilities: Innate Spellcasting: At will — detect thoughts, levitate
Mind Blast: Recharge 5-6

monsters.name: Beholder
monsters.tactics: Paranoid, territorial, intelligent. Uses Antimagic Cone to shut down spellcasters while using eye rays on others. Targets healers and casters first. Creates lairs with kill zones.

attacks.name: Eye Rays
attacks.hit: Random 1-10, each targets one creature within 120 ft
attacks.damage: Varies by ray (charm, paralyze, fear, slow, enervation, telekinetic, sleep, petrification, disintegration, death)

specialAbilities: Antimagic Cone: 150-foot cone suppresses magic
Eye Rays: 3 random rays per turn, no repeat
Legendary Actions: Eye Ray (cost 1), 3/round

monsters.name: Adult Red Dragon
monsters.tactics: The most vain and destructive of chromatic dragons. Opens with breath weapon on the largest group. Uses Frightful Presence to scatter. Picks off isolated targets with legendary actions. Prefers to fight in its lair where it has lair actions.
specialAbilities: Legendary Resistance: 3/day, auto-fail a save
Frightful Presence: DC 19 Wis save or frightened for 1 minute
Legendary Actions: Detect, Tail Attack, Wing Attack (cost 2)

monsters.name: Lich
monsters.tactics: Master planner who never engages directly without preparation. Leads with crowd control, uses legendary actions to disrupt casters. Phylactery resurrection means the party must find and destroy it to win permanently.
immunities: Poison, Psychic; Bludgeoning/Piercing/Slashing from nonmagical weapons

attacks.name: Paralyzing Touch
attacks.damage: 3d6 cold (DC 15 Con save or paralyzed for 1 minute)

specialAbilities: Legendary Resistance: 3/day
Spellcasting: 9th-level caster, Spell save DC 20
Rejuvenation: Returns 1d10 days after destruction if phylactery intact
Legendary Actions: Cantrip, Paralyzing Touch (cost 2), Frightening Gaze (cost 2), Disrupt Life (cost 3)

monsters.name: Bandit
monsters.tactics: Attacks from ambush on roads. Prefers to intimidate and rob rather than kill. Retreats if half their number fall.

monsters.name: Hobgoblin
monsters.tactics: Disciplined military tactics. Uses Martial Advantage (+2d6) when ally is adjacent to target. Fights in formation. Leaders direct lower-ranking troops.
specialAbilities: Martial Advantage: +2d6 damage when ally is within 5 ft of target and ally isn't incapacitated

monsters.name: Worg
monsters.tactics: Goblin cavalry mount. Uses speed to flank and knock targets prone. Often ridden by goblins who attack from its back.

attacks.name: Bite
attacks.damage: 2d6 + 3 piercing (target prone if Large or smaller, DC 13 Str save)

specialAbilities: Keen Hearing and Smell: Advantage on Perception checks using hearing or smell

monsters.name: Gelatinous Cube
monsters.tactics: Nearly invisible ambush predator in dungeons. Engulfs characters who walk into it. Acid damage slowly dissolves equipment and flesh.
immunities: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Exhaustion, Frightened, Prone
specialAbilities: Engulf: DC 12 Dex save or engulfed, restrained, take 3d6 acid per turn
Transparent: DC 15 Perception to notice before walking into it
Ooze Cube: Engulfed creatures are blinded and restrained

metadata.disclaimer: Monster stats are summaries for quick reference. Consult the full SRD or Monster Manual for complete stat blocks and lore. D&D and its rules are trademarks of Wizards of the Coast.
